title: CRS IF10183
text: Corroborating conduit for the Section 3 FY2023 foreign-assistance obligations total of approximately $99.9B. Section 1 Section 3 Corroborates the published FY2023 foreign-assistance obligations figure used in Section 3. Supports the audit's separation between obligations and disbursements.

title: 8 U.S.C. Sec. 1641(b)
text: Qualified-alien definition used throughout the domestic eligibility framework. Section 2 Section 8 Section 9 Defines the lawful-status categories used in domestic-program analysis. Supports the rule against collapsing different immigration-status categories into one figure.

title: 8 U.S.C. Sec. 1611
text: Federal public-benefit restriction baseline for non-qualified aliens, including the emergency-medical exception structure. Section 2 Section 7 Section 8 Section 9 Supports the default ineligibility baseline for undocumented immigrants in most federal benefits. Provides the emergency-medical exception framework carried into Section 7. A-037

title: 8 U.S.C. Sec. 1613
text: Five-year bar and related exceptions for federal means-tested public benefits. Section 2 Section 8 Section 9 Supports the methodology distinction between qualified categories and waiting-period restrictions.

title: GAO Budget Glossary (GAO-05-734SP)
text: Primary budget-accounting terminology for appropriation, obligation, outlay, and number-type separation. Section 2 Section 3 Defines appropriation, obligation, and budget authority for the audit's accounting rules. Supports the rule that unlike number types cannot be merged.

title: CBO Common Budgetary Terms Explained
text: Companion budgetary definitions used to corroborate number-type discipline throughout the publication. Section 2 Reinforces the audit's separation of appropriations, obligations, outlays, and projections.

title: ForeignAssistance.gov financial datasets
text: Primary aggregate dataset for foreign-assistance obligations and disbursements used in Section 3. Section 1 Section 3 Section 6 Supports the FY2023 obligations figure of approximately $99.9B. Supports the FY2023 disbursements figure of approximately $71.9B. Supports the FY2024 obligations figure of approximately $85.8B and later reported-to-date figures. A-005

title: Pew foreign-aid reporting summary
text: Corroborating conduit for FY2023 foreign-assistance disbursements, agency composition, and military-share context. Section 3 Corroborates the FY2023 disbursement total near $71.9B. Corroborates agency and military-share breakdowns used in Section 3. A-005

title: USAFacts foreign-aid reporting summary
text: Corroborating conduit for later-year obligations in the international-assistance lane. Section 3 Corroborates approximately $85.8B FY2024 obligations and the lower-confidence FY2025 to-date and FY2026 to-date figures. A-005

title: State Supplementary Tables FY2025
text: Account-level foreign-assistance tables retained for next-pass pulls and account composition checks. Section 3 Supports account-level breakdowns behind the international-assistance universe.

title: CRS IF11437
text: Used to distinguish taxpayer-funded military aid from allied-funded foreign military sales. Section 3 Section 4 Section 5 Supports exclusion of allied-funded FMS from taxpayer totals. Supports the Section 3 to Section 5 netting rule around military-aid overlap. A-017

title: UkraineOversight.gov
text: Primary oversight source for Ukraine appropriated-versus-disbursed context used in the illustrative Section 4 lane. Section 4 Supports the publication's Ukraine stage and delivery-context framing without making Section 4 additive to the totals.

title: State Department Ukraine/Israel aid reporting
text: Primary State reporting used in the illustrative Section 4 examples for identifiable aid flows. Section 4 Section 5 Supports identifiable Ukraine and Israel aid lines discussed in Section 4.

title: GAO drawdown valuation reporting
text: Used for the reported drawdown overvaluation issue and to keep transfer value separate from replacement appropriations. Section 4 Section 5 Supports the GAO-reported drawdown misvaluation issue. Supports excluding drawdown transfer value from net-new taxpayer replacement totals.

title: CEPR Ukraine valuation context
text: Retained as disconfirming or bounding context for delivered-value discussions in Section 4. Section 4 Provides lower-bound contextual estimates for value reaching Ukraine without being adopted as the canonical total.

title: Council on Foreign Relations aid tracker context
text: Used as contextual corroboration for Section 4's illustrative examples. Section 4 Corroborates broad Ukraine and Israel aid-stage context without serving as the primary figure source.

title: CRS RL33222
text: Primary congressional source for Israel FMF, missile-defense, and OSP context used in Section 4. Section 4 Supports the identifiable Israel FMF and missile-defense appropriations discussed in Section 4. Supports the offshore-procurement share used in the Article 15 beneficiary discussion.

title: DSCA SAMM and related security-cooperation materials
text: Program framework source for marginal or pending security-cooperation lanes surrounding Section 5 Block B. Section 5 Supports scoping and exclusion treatment for marginal security-cooperation mechanisms around the recurring annual lane.

title: State FMTR
text: Foreign Military Training Report used as supporting context in the military-aid lane. Section 5 Supports military-assistance program context and classification in Section 5.

title: CRS IF12040
text: Component-level CRS source for Ukraine security-assistance figures used in Section 5 reconciliation. Section 5 Supports the component-level USAI and PDA-replacement reconciliation in Section 5.

title: CRS IN12453
text: CRS source for cumulative PDA replacement and related Ukraine security-assistance figures. Section 5 Supports the cumulative PDA-replacement appropriation and obligation figures used in Block A.

title: CRS R48182
text: CRS source for USAI and related Ukraine military-assistance figures used in Block A. Section 5 Supports the USAI available and obligated figures used in the Block A subtotal.

title: GAO-23-105842
text: Primary source for the Section 333 Build Partner Capacity historical figure used in the recurring annual lane. Section 5 Section 14 Supports the approximately $5.6B FY2018-2022 Section 333 figure and the annualized recurring lane near $1.1B.

title: GAO-24-107232
text: GAO source for the FY2022-2023 subset of PDA replacement figures retained in reconciliation. Section 5 Supports the narrower FY2022-2023 subset retained alongside the larger cumulative PDA replacement total.

title: DoD / DSCA infographic
text: DoD summary used to verify separation between USAI and PDA replacement lines in Section 5. Section 5 Supports the non-overlap of USAI and PDA replacement lines. Supports the cumulative military-assistance line items used in Block A.

title: DoD CTEF J-Books
text: Primary source for the Counter-ISIS Train and Equip Fund figure used in the recurring annual lane. Section 5 Section 14 Supports the approximately $0.5B recurring annual CTEF lane.

title: 10 U.S.C. Sec. 333
text: Statutory authority for the Section 333 Build Partner Capacity lane. Section 5 Supports the legal basis and lane identity for Section 333 funding in Section 5.

title: ORR program documents
text: Program documents used to identify ORR base program lines such as RSS and T&MS in Section 6. Section 6 Section 14 Supports the FY2024 base figures for Refugee Support Services and Transitional and Medical Services. Supports the program-level structure of the ORR lane. A-018

title: ORR entrant-assistance program materials
text: Supporting ORR materials for entrant cash, medical, or service pathways discussed in Section 6. Section 6 Supports the routed treatment of entrant-directed cash and medical assistance inside the ORR lane. A-018

title: USAspending account 075-1503
text: Federal account reference for Refugee and Entrant Assistance used to anchor the Section 6 lane. Section 6 Section 14 Supports the identification of Refugee and Entrant Assistance as the central federal account in Section 6. A-018

title: ORR service-delivery and provider-route materials
text: Supporting ORR material for provider, state, and voluntary-agency delivery pathways in Section 6. Section 6 Supports the conclusion that a large share of ORR dollars is routed through states, operators, and voluntary agencies rather than directly as cash. A-018

title: KFF emergency Medicaid analysis
text: Corroborating source for the Section 7 emergency Medicaid figure and the provider-capture framing. Section 7 Section 14 Section 16 Corroborates the approximately $3.8B FY2023 emergency Medicaid figure. Supports the explanation that emergency Medicaid reimburses hospitals rather than paying cash to patients. A-037

title: CMS-64 / MACPAC emergency Medicaid reporting
text: Primary source of record for the Section 7 emergency Medicaid figure. Section 7 Supports the approximately $3.8B FY2023 emergency Medicaid figure on a federal-plus-state basis. A-037

title: CRS R46462
text: Primary structural source for housing-program eligibility, Section 214 limits, and status-verification rules. Section 10 Supports the claim that major HUD rental-assistance programs are limited to citizens and eligible noncitizens under Section 214.

title: HUD program guidance
text: HUD guidance used alongside CRS to explain proration and status-verification rules in Section 10. Section 10 Supports proration treatment under mixed-status housing households and the exclusion of ineligible members.

title: Plyler / federal education-aid legal framework
text: Primary legal framework for status-blind K-12 access and federal student-aid eligibility boundaries used in Section 11. Section 11 Supports the claim that K-12 schooling is status-blind under Plyler v. Doe. Supports the claim that undocumented students are barred from federal student aid while eligible noncitizen breakout data is not published.

title: CRS R47936
text: Appropriations source for Refugee and Entrant Assistance and related ORR emergency funding in Section 6. Section 6 Section 14 Supports the approximately $4.2B FY2023 emergency-designated Refugee and Entrant Assistance figure. Supports the approximately $481M FY2024 emergency appropriation figure. A-018

title: ORR / HHS additional resettlement support materials
text: Supporting ORR documentation used to map the service and provider structure of Section 6. Section 6 Supports the ownership map that keeps ORR-routed assistance inside Section 6 rather than duplicating it elsewhere. A-018

title: SSA SSI program totals
text: Primary SSI program totals used in Section 9 for the overall payment baseline and average payment amount. Section 9 Supports approximately $61B in SSI total payments in FY/CY2023. Supports the average monthly SSI payment baseline used in the Section 9 calculation. A-028

title: SSA noncitizen SSI data
text: Primary source for the noncitizen SSI recipient count used in Section 9. Section 1 Section 9 Section 14 Supports the published noncitizen SSI recipient count of 365,714 in December 2021. Supports the computed approximately $2.21B annualized SSI line on the December 2021 basis. A-028

title: USDA-FNS SNAP characteristics data
text: Primary SNAP program and participant-composition data used in Section 8. Section 8 Section 16 Supports the FY2023 SNAP participation figures used in Section 8. Supports the composition finding that approximately 89.4 percent of participants are U.S.-born citizens and approximately 3.3 percent are other noncitizens.

title: ACF TANF totals and characteristics
text: Published TANF totals and characteristics used only to frame why TANF remains largely routed to Section 13. Section 9 Supports the claim that TANF totals are published but noncitizen-attributable benefit dollars are not cleanly isolated.

title: Cato SIPP-modeled welfare context
text: Directional, non-canonical context retained in Section 9 but not adopted as a Total A figure. Section 9 Provides modeled context for noncitizen welfare use while remaining outside the audit's adopted topline because of scope and methodology mismatches.

title: HUD ineligible-tenant reporting
text: Closest primary figure used in Section 10 for ineligible individuals inside assisted housing structures. Section 10 Supports the heavily caveated closest primary housing figure discussed in Section 10.

title: CBO emergency Medicaid cost reporting
text: CBO time-window context for emergency Medicaid, retained in reconciliation but not as a competing annual figure. Section 7 Supports the publication's time-window reconciliation showing why multi-year CBO totals do not compete with the FY2023 figure. A-037

title: House Budget Committee emergency Medicaid context
text: Subset-period emergency Medicaid context retained in Table 14-H reconciliation. Section 7 Supports the claim that larger emergency Medicaid numbers cited elsewhere are different-period figures rather than contradictions of the FY2023 annual figure. A-037

title: CBO immigration-surge projection context
text: Forward-looking context explicitly kept out of adopted totals because it is a projection and includes out-of-scope categories. Section 7 Supports the publication's explicit distinction between current-year measured figures and future projections.

title: Unpublished U.S.-capture share in international assistance
text: Without a published capture-share breakout, the site can report gross taxpayer assistance (Total A) but cannot compute a defensible Total B for international assistance. Section 3 states that Total B is materially below Total A, but not measurable from the current public record. Program-level reporting that distinguishes value captured by U.S. entities from value reaching non-U.S. recipients. Section 1 Section 3 S-038 S-039 S-040

title: Cross-section harmonization between foreign-assistance and military-aid lanes
text: Section 3 uses single-year obligations while Section 5 uses cumulative multi-year military lanes, so a blended headline would overstate precision. The site preserves separate lanes and explicitly forbids a synthetic grand total. A lawful harmonization method that keeps measure type, period, and duplication risks separated. Section 5 S-043

title: ORR consolidated outlay and recipient/provider split
text: The refugee-resettlement lane is routed through states, voluntary agencies, and providers, but the public record does not cleanly isolate value reaching entrants. Section 6 names the program and the gap, but leaves Total B unresolved. Public ORR outlay reporting that isolates entrant-directed value versus provider or operator capture. Section 1 Section 6 S-060 S-062 S-070

title: SSI point-in-time basis versus annual flow basis
text: The SSI figure is computed from a published December 2021 basis, which is strong for that point in time but not a full annual transactional ledger. Section 9 keeps the figure labeled on its Dec. 2021 basis and avoids overstating it as a harmonized annual program total. A published annual SSI spending breakout for noncitizen recipients or a fully reconciled monthly series. Section 1 Section 9 S-072 S-073

title: Federal-only emergency Medicaid share
text: The site can publish the federal-plus-state emergency Medicaid figure used in the locked section, but not the federal-only share needed for a cleaner federal total. The limitation is carried forward explicitly in the Executive Summary and Section 7. A public federal-only emergency Medicaid spending breakout for the same period and population basis. Section 1 Section 7 S-003 S-064 S-065 S-078 S-079

title: Gross, not net
text: Every adopted figure is gross money directed toward non-U.S. recipients or noncitizens. The audit does not offset those figures against taxes paid or any net-fiscal-impact model. Section 1 Section 2 Section 9

title: Status categories are never collapsed
text: Lawful permanent residents, refugees, asylees, parolees, temporary statuses, and undocumented persons are analytically distinct categories and are never merged into one undifferentiated headline. Section 2

title: Number type and resource category are recorded separately
text: Each figure keeps both its number type and its resource category visible. Drawdown transfer value, replacement appropriations, obligations, outlays, and program authorities are tracked as different things. Section 2 Section 5

title: Universe-of-programs completeness artifact
text: Sections with broad program scope publish their universe explicitly, including residual or immaterial lines, so no material account is silently dropped from the research frame. Section 3 Section 4 Section 6

title: The Two Running Totals
text: The audit preserves two permanent running concepts: Total A for gross directed resources and Total B for estimated value ultimately reaching the non-U.S. recipient after U.S.-captured share. They are never merged. Section 3 Section 14

title: Section 3 to Section 5 netting rule
text: Military-aid lines already embedded in the foreign-assistance aggregate are counted once. Section 5 aggregates only NET-NEW military lanes and excludes allied-funded FMS, DCS, and other non-taxpayer or already-counted amounts. Section 3 Section 4 Section 5 Section 14

title: Research docket publication rule
text: Illustrative sections may publish their search path, source classes, and unresolved pulls so readers can see what was investigated even when those sections are non-additive. Section 4

title: Standardized key-findings opening
text: Certain locked sections begin with a standardized key-findings block that summarizes already-established evidence without introducing new evidence or new methodology in the summary itself. Section 4 Section 5

title: No blended grand total
text: Where lane subtotals sit on incompatible bases such as disbursement versus obligation, annual versus cumulative, or federal versus federal-plus-state, the rigorous output is a set of basis-segregated subtotals rather than one synthetic total. Section 1 Section 5 Section 12 Section 14 Section 16

title: Ownership map for domestic sections
text: Domestic federal dollars are owned by one canonical section so routed programs are not counted twice. ORR, Medicaid, TANF-related pathways, and state-administered lenses keep their owning section explicit. Section 6 Section 7 Section 8 Section 9 Section 10 Section 11 Section 12 Section 13

title: Section 14 blueprint frozen
text: The conservative-total section is built to a fixed blueprint that preserves lane separation, forbids invented gap estimates, and requires reproducibility from the locked source sections. Section 14

title: Section 14 locked
text: After the blueprint was populated and checked, Section 14 was locked as the publication's conservative set-of-subtotals representation. Section 14

title: Section 15 locked
text: The limitations section was locked to carry forward the publication's missing-record constraints without backfilling them with modeled certainty. Section 15

title: Section 16 locked
text: The final argument was locked after assembly so the publication's closing synthesis reflects the same basis-separation and limitation rules as the rest of Version 1.0. Section 16

title: Repository completion assembly
text: Repository completion for Version 1.0 means the locked sections, appendices, source library, and decision log are assembled as publication assets without rewriting the underlying analytical conclusions. Appendix A Appendix B Repository assets

title: Executive Summary
text: known minimum incompatible bases no blended grand total noncitizen SSI emergency Medicaid open questions A-005 A-018 A-028 A-037

title: Definitions and Methodology
text: recipient economic beneficiary appropriation obligation outlay evidence class confidence estimation standard category separation

title: International Assistance
text: ForeignAssistance.gov obligations disbursements Total A Total B A-005

title: Military Aid
text: USAI PDA replacement Section 333 CTEF A-017 drawdown replacement

title: Medicaid / Emergency Medical
text: emergency Medicaid A-037 provider capture federal-only share

title: Food Assistance
text: SNAP no modeled share citizen-child exclusion Section 13
